meta.description: Build LASSO logistic regression models for simultaneous feature selection and binary classification. Inputs: feature matrix, binary outcome labels. Outputs: selected feature list, model coefficients, lambda cross-validation plot, ROC performance curve.

key_strengths: The skill is fully runnable end to end with a clear CLI, packaged sample data, and passing automated tests.
Error handling is disciplined: parameter validation, structured SKILL_* codes, timeout control, atomic file writes, and reproducibility controls are all present.
Progressive disclosure is well executed, keeping SKILL.md readable while placing detail in references and modular R scripts.
Dynamic behavior closely matches the documentation, including the canonical coefficient snapshot and graceful handling of missing features.

recommendations.title: Add explicit routing section
recommendations.problem: The skill does not expose a dedicated When to Use / When Not to Use section or an explicit execution-mode declaration. Automated routers can still infer intent, but less reliably than humans.
recommendations.root_cause: Routing signals are distributed across frontmatter, usage examples, and workflow prose instead of being centralized in one parser-friendly section.
recommendations.fix: Add short When to Use and When Not to Use sections near the top of SKILL.md. Also add a single-line execution-mode note such as 'Execution Mode: CLI via Rscript scripts/main.R'.

recommendations.title: Expand regression tests for edge paths
recommendations.problem: Manual audit confirmed that invalid nfolds and partial feature-panel mismatches behave correctly, but there are no dedicated regression tests for those exact CLI paths.
recommendations.root_cause: Current tests focus on core reproducibility and a small subset of validation behavior.
recommendations.fix: Add one test that asserts invalid nfolds fails with the documented SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER message and one test that verifies missing_features.txt is written when some requested features are absent.

recommendations.title: Document sample-level data sensitivity
recommendations.problem: The skill writes feature_matrix.csv with sample-level data by default but does not explicitly warn users about handling sensitive cohorts.
recommendations.root_cause: The current documentation emphasizes format and outputs more than downstream data-governance considerations.
recommendations.fix: Add a short note in SKILL.md and the CLI guide stating that feature_matrix.csv may contain sensitive sample-level data and should be stored or shared according to the project's data-governance rules.
